feature1_1_empty_input.json.description: Parsing an input that is absent, empty, or composed only of whitespace yields the empty-collection sentinel rather than a list of records. The output is the textual empty-object form.

feature1_2_records.json.description: Parsing a textual block of one or more records, where records are separated by a blank line and each record is a set of `key: value` lines, produces an ordered list of key/value maps. Record order and per-record key order follow the input; records need not share the same keys, and any blank lines trailing the final record are ignored.

input.input:
device: /dev/disk1
description: Macintosh HD
size: 249.8 GB
mountpoint: /

device: /dev/disk1
description: Macintosh HD
size: 249.8 GB
mountpoint: /

device: /dev/disk2
description: elementary OS
size: 15.7 GB
mountpoint: /Volumes/Elementary

feature1_3_trailing_commas.json.description: When a value ends with one trailing comma, that comma is stripped from the parsed value while internal commas are preserved.

feature1_4_null_values.json.description: A line that declares a key with no value, or a bare line with no `key: value` separator at all, parses to that key mapped to the null sentinel. The remaining text becomes the key verbatim, including embedded spaces.

feature2_1_platform_enumeration.json.description: Enumerating devices for a recognized operating-system platform routes to that platform's collection step, captures its raw textual output, and returns the parsed list of device records. The same parsing contract applies regardless of platform; only the supplied raw output differs.

input.output:
device: "/dev/sda"
description: "My drive"
size: "15 GB"
mountpoint: "/mnt/drive"

device: "\\\\.\\PHYSICALDRIVE1"
description: "My drive"
size: "15 GB"
mountpoint: "D:"

feature2_2_unsupported_platform.json.description: Requesting enumeration for a platform the toolkit does not support fails up front with a neutral, language-independent error category and the offending platform name carried in its own field.

_constraints.output_rule: Returns canonical JSON followed by a trailing newline.

_constraints.input_condition: input is absent, empty, or whitespace-only
input contains one or more records
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a JSON array of device records

_constraints.input_condition: record contains a value ending with a single comma
_constraints.output_rule: Strips only the trailing comma from the value
Maps the key to the null sentinel (JSON null)
Treats the entire line text as the key and maps it to the null sentinel

_constraints.input_condition: input contains multiple records separated by blank lines
_constraints.output_rule: Returns an ordered JSON array preserving record order; trailing blank lines in input are ignored
Parses the provided 'output' string and returns the result as a JSON array with a trailing newline
Returns a two-line error format: 'error=unsupported_platform' followed by 'platform=<name>', then a trailing newline
Routes to platform collection, captures raw output, parses it using the parse contract

_constraints.input_condition: raw text lines with quoted values
_constraints.output_rule: Quietly unquotes values during parsing; literal values are preserved in the JSON output without quotes
Outputs exactly the raw stdout from the program under test to the named output file
